title: frangula pubescens
intro: Frangula pubescens, also known as the purple ash or alder buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides, which have been used to treat various gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Frangula pubescens include its ability to stimulate bowel movements, relieve constipation, and help treat conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and hemorrhoids. Frangula pubescens is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs in herbal remedies to enhance its medicinal effects.
